title: Release Tracker Schema
description: Machine-readable schema for Release Tracker application data model. Used for building translation adapters to other backend ticketing systems. The major version tracks SCHEMA_VERSION in src/types.ts (guarded by src/schema.test.ts); the wire contract between the app and the sync service lives separately in packages/sync-contract/openapi.yaml.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-created members.

nonContributing.description: When true, excluded from capacity calculations (e.g. EMs, PMs who don't contribute velocity).

ItemType.description: Work item type supplied by the connector (e.g. Bug, Story, Task). Read-only in the app.

id.description: Connector-assigned type ID; null if the connector doesn't expose one. Preserved for future item creation.

Team.description: A team that owns releases. Defines baseline velocity used to calculate sprint capacity.

velocity.description: Points per 2-week sprint (baseline, no days off). Used to compute sprint capacity.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-created teams.

WorkStream.description: A logical work category within a release (e.g. 'Checkout API', 'Search Revamp'). Work items are organized into streams; streams are displayed as columns in sprint and work-stream views.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-created work streams.

engineersRequired.description: App-owned enrichment: engineers the stream needs to progress. Drives the capacity-fit health forecast. Survives connector sync. Null when not configured.

planningMuted.description: App-owned enrichment: the user has muted the planning-runway proactive-creation alarm for this stream (e.g. tickets deferred while research is pending). Silences the alarm only — the stream still reads as un-judgeable, never green, when it has unclaimed runway. Survives connector sync. Defaults to false.

build.description: Connector-owned provenance: the build/release this stream was carried in from, when it differs from this release. Null = native to this release. Drives the 'on-build only' lens.

externalUrl.description: Connector-owned deep link: absolute URL to this work stream (epic) in the external system, opened in a new tab. Null for locally-created streams or when the backend exposes no addressable page.

attributes.description: Connector vocabulary values for this stream. Optional — absence means none.

codeFreezeISO.description: App-owned enrichment: overrides the release's code freeze for this stream alone (e.g. an infra stream that must lock down ahead of feature streams). Optional — absence/null means it inherits Release.codeFreezeISO. Survives connector sync.

Sprint.description: A time period within a release. Local releases default to 8 two-week sprints; connector releases take their sprint structure from the external system and may have any count or length.

id.description: Unique sprint identifier. Display order is determined by startISO ascending, not a positional index.

name.description: Sprint name (e.g. 'Sprint 1'); for connector releases the name comes from the external system.

endISO.description: Sprint end date (ISO 8601, inclusive; typically 13 days after start for standard 2-week sprints)

daysOff.description: Number of person-days the team is unavailable during this sprint (e.g. holidays, planned leave). Used to reduce sprint capacity. This is an aggregate count, not a list of dates.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-managed sprints.

plannedVelocity.description: App-owned point-in-time baseline (in points) of the sprint's planned velocity — the capacity-adjusted sprintVel frozen when the sprint started. Null = not yet frozen (future sprint), planned velocity derives live from team.velocity. Once a sprint begins it is stamped and becomes immutable so later velocity edits never rewrite a started sprint's commitment. Survives connector sync (never set by the connector).

Event.description: A milestone or calendar marker within a release (e.g. 'Code freeze', 'GA'). Events are rendered as badges in the sprint row.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-created events.

AttributeBag.description: Connector vocabulary values keyed by the connector catalog's FieldSpec.key — fields the catalog declares that don't map to a canonical concept (e.g. a Bug's severity). Scalars only. Connector-owned; writeable keys (per the catalog) are locally editable and push back.

ConnectorItemType.description: One work-item type from the connector's catalog (id, label, fields[] of data-only FieldSpecs). The authoritative schema is ConnectorItemType in packages/sync-contract/openapi.yaml; stored here as an opaque snapshot.

StatusDef.description: One native workflow state of the connector's backend, mapped onto a canonical Status category. The connector's full list is its status vocabulary: edit options + push validation. Authoritative schema: StatusDef in packages/sync-contract/openapi.yaml.

ReleaseCatalog.description: Sync-time snapshot of a connector's vocabulary: item-type catalog + status vocabulary + work-stream field catalog. Stored on the release so synced data stays interpretable offline.

workStreamFields.description: The connector's work-stream field catalog (data-only FieldSpecs describing WorkStream.attributes; flat — streams have no type dimension). Authoritative schema: ConnectorMeta.workStreamFields in packages/sync-contract/openapi.yaml; stored here as an opaque snapshot. Fields with filterable:true become stream-level facets.

ReleaseConnector.description: A release's binding to an external system. Holds only non-secret routing params (e.g. which project/board/version to pull). Credentials live in the local sync service, never here. A 'Local' release has connector === null and never syncs.

type.description: Connector type identifier (e.g. 'acme'). The set of valid values is defined by the local sync service.

config.description: Non-secret routing parameters for the connector (e.g. { boardId: '42', projectKey: 'PROJ' }).

SyncStatus.description: Per-release sync state, surfaced in the UI (badge + toast).

lastISO.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last completed sync attempt. Null if no sync has run yet.

state.description: Current sync state: 'idle' before first sync, 'ok' after a successful sync, 'error' after a failed sync.

message.description: Human-readable status or error message from the last sync. Null when not applicable.

WorkItem.description: A unit of work within a release. Belongs to at most one work stream and at most one sprint at a time; null means unassigned/backlog.

releaseId.description: ID of the release this item belongs to

workStreamId.description: ID of the work stream this item belongs to, or null if unassigned to a stream

sprintId.description: ID of the sprint containing this item, or null for the backlog (unscheduled)

key.description: Human-readable key (e.g. 'PROJ-100'). Format: release prefix (3 letters max) + hyphen + sequential number.

description.description: Detailed description. Format is indicated by descriptionFormat.

descriptionFormat.description: Format of the description field. 'text' (or absent) means plain text; 'html' means sanitized HTML supplied by a connector. Optional — absence implies 'text'.

points.description: Story points estimate. Null means unestimated. Used to compute velocity and capacity.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-created items.

assignedMemberId.description: ID of the team member assigned to this item, or null if unassigned. References Member.id.

build.description: Build/release label this item originated from, if it was pulled from a prior release by the connector. Null for locally-created items. Read-only.

externalUrl.description: Connector-owned deep link: absolute URL to this item in the external system (e.g. its issue page), opened in a new tab. Null for locally-created items or when the backend exposes no addressable page. Read-only.

dirtyFields.description: Names of fields edited locally since the last sync/push, awaiting push to the external system. Empty array for clean (unmodified) items.

syncedValues.description: Last value seen from the connector for this item's writeable fields, keyed by the same local field names used in dirtyFields: 'points' (number), 'sprint' (local sprintId or null), and any writeable vocabulary keys (FieldSpec.key). The baseline a dirty edit diverges from; used to preview a pending push (old → new) and to revert an item to its synced value. Null for locally-created items. Optional — absence implies null.

itemType.description: Work item type (Bug, Story, Task, etc.) from the connector. Read-only. Null for locally-created items or when the connector does not supply a type.

attributes.description: Connector vocabulary values for this item, interpreted via the release's catalog snapshot. Writeable keys (per the catalog) are locally editable and push back; the rest are read-only. Optional — absence means none.

statusNative.description: The item's native workflow state (denormalized id + label, mirrors itemType). `status` carries this state's canonical category — what all derivations compute over; this adds the backend's real state for display/edit. Pushable (as statusId) when the item type declares a writeable status field. Null/absent for local items or backends without a richer workflow.

Release.description: A release cycle. Local releases default to 8 two-week sprints (16 weeks); connector releases have their sprint count and structure determined by the external system. Organized into work streams; contains work items.

startISO.description: Release start date (ISO 8601). First sprint starts on this date.

teamId.description: ID of the team owning this release

sprints.description: Sprints in this release, ordered by startISO ascending. Local releases default to 8 two-week sprints; connector releases may have any count or duration.

codeFreezeISO.description: App-owned code check-in deadline. Null = defaults to the last sprint's endISO (no artificial cutoff). Feeds the capacity-fit forecast/burndown and renders as a critical-tone calendar chip on the sprint whose range contains it. Individual work streams may override via WorkStream.codeFreezeISO.

externalId.description: ID from external ticketing system (Acme, Linear, etc.). Null for locally-created releases.

connector.description: Connector binding for this release. Null for local releases that do not sync to an external system.

sync.description: Last-known sync state for this release. Null for local releases or before the first sync has run.

sprintLengthDays.description: Uniform calendar length (days) of every sprint in this release, chosen at creation and not editable. Drives sprint generation for local releases (e.g. 14 for a 2-week cadence). Connector releases take their sprints from the external system and may vary in length, so this holds a nominal value only.

catalog.description: Snapshot of the connector's vocabulary (item-type catalog + status vocabulary + work-stream field catalog), taken at each sync. Lets the app interpret synced items' and streams' attributes and native statuses (labels, enum options, writeability, filterability) offline, and keeps already-synced items stable if the live catalog changes. Null/absent = never synced or a local release.

pendingMemberOverrides.description: Transient, one-shot reattach hint set only on a release freshly imported from a share link: the sharer's app-owned nonContributing flag per synced member, keyed by member externalId. The team is unbound at import time, so these wait here until the first sync creates the members and seeds their flag (over the connector's value), then are cleared. Absent otherwise.

MemberOverride.description: A team member's app-owned nonContributing flag, keyed by connector externalId. Travels in a share link (see Release.pendingMemberOverrides) and reattaches on the recipient's first sync.

externalId.description: External member id this override applies to.

nonContributing.description: The sharer's app-owned nonContributing value to seed on first sync.

AppState.description: Root application state. Persists to localStorage.

items.description: All work items across all releases

lastSyncISO.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last sync with backend

SCHEMA_VERSION.description: Current schema version for localStorage persistence

DEFAULT_SPRINT_COUNT.description: Default number of sprints for a locally-created release. Connector releases may have any count.

SPRINT_LENGTH_DAYS.description: Default sprint duration in calendar days for a locally-created release (2-week cadence). The actual length is chosen at creation and stored per release as Release.sprintLengthDays; connector sprints may vary.

WORKDAYS_PER_SPRINT.description: Nominal working days per standard 2-week sprint (Mon–Fri, no holidays). Capacity uses workdaysInRange() over a sprint's actual date range to support variable-length connector sprints.

sprintCapacity.description: Planned capacity for a sprint in story points. Accounts for team velocity and days off. Full capacity uses the sprint's actual business days (Mon–Fri within [startISO, endISO]) so variable-length connector sprints compute correctly; a standard 14-day sprint yields 10. Only contributing members (nonContributing === false) count toward fullCap.
sprintCapacity.formula: fullCap = contributingMembers.length * workdaysInRange(sprint.startISO, sprint.endISO); sprintCapacity = round(team.velocity * ((fullCap - sprint.daysOff) / fullCap))

example.scenario: Team of 5 contributing members, velocity 40, standard 14-day sprint (10 workdays → fullCap 50), 10 person-days off

activeSprint.description: The sprint whose date range contains today (startISO <= today <= endISO). Used to highlight the current sprint in the release plan view.
activeSprint.note: If today falls outside all sprint ranges, no sprint is active.

releaseCompletion.description: Percentage of work items in a release with status 'Complete'.
releaseCompletion.formula: releaseCompletion = (count of items with status='Complete') / (total count of items) * 100

statusSegments.description: For a set of work items, count items per status for rendering a segmented bar.

eventsInSprint.description: Filter release events to those whose dateISO falls within [sprint.startISO, sprint.endISO]. Sorted by dateISO ascending.

Team → Release.description: A team owns zero or more releases. teamId on Release points to Team.

Release → WorkStream.description: A release contains zero or more work streams. Embedded as Release.workStreams[].

Release → Event.description: A release has zero or more events. Embedded as Release.events[].

Release → Sprint.description: A release has zero or more sprints. Embedded as Release.sprints[]. Local releases default to 8 fixed two-week sprints; connector releases start empty and have sprints created from the external system on sync. Display order is by startISO ascending.

Release → WorkItem.description: A release contains one or more work items. WorkItem.releaseId points to Release.

WorkStream → WorkItem.description: A work stream contains zero or more work items. WorkItem.workStreamId points to WorkStream; null means the item is not assigned to any stream.

Sprint → WorkItem.description: A sprint contains zero or more work items. WorkItem.sprintId points to Sprint.id within the same Release; null means the item is in the backlog (unscheduled).

Member → WorkItem.description: A member may be assigned to zero or more work items. WorkItem.assignedMemberId points to Member.id within the release's team; null means unassigned.

adaptationGuide.overview: This schema describes the Release Tracker data model. Use it to build adapters that translate between Release Tracker and other backend ticketing systems (Acme, Linear, Azure DevOps, etc.).

keyMapping.Release Tracker Release: Maps to a project, epic, or initiative in target system
keyMapping.Release Tracker WorkStream: Maps to a component, team, or feature area in target system
keyMapping.Release Tracker WorkItem: Maps to an issue/task/story in target system
keyMapping.Release Tracker Sprint: Maps to a sprint/iteration in target system

notes: WorkItem.key is auto-generated (e.g. 'PROJ-100'). If importing from a target system, preserve the original external ID in externalId and decide whether to remap the key.
Local releases default to 8 two-week (14-day) sprints; connector releases may have any sprint count or duration — do not assume a fixed count or length.

Status enum is fixed ('Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Under Review', 'Blocked', 'Complete') — these are CATEGORIES the app's derivations compute over. A connector with a richer workflow declares its native states (StatusDef vocabulary, mapped onto categories) and tags items with statusNative; the app displays the native label while computing on the category.

WorkItem.attributes / WorkStream.attributes carry connector vocabulary (catalog-declared fields that map to no canonical concept, e.g. a Bug's severity), keyed by the catalog's FieldSpec.key. The service filters/coerces them at the boundary; writeable keys edit locally and push back via PushItemChange.fields.attributes.

Release.catalog is the sync-time snapshot of the connector's itemTypes catalog — the authority for interpreting attributes offline. See packages/sync-contract/openapi.yaml for the authoritative FieldSpec shape.

WorkItem.syncedValues is a record keyed by local dirty-field names ('points', 'sprint', vocabulary keys) holding the last synced value of each writeable field — the baseline for push previews and reverts.
Points are integers with no ceiling; adapt to target system's point scale if different.
Team.velocity is a constant used for capacity planning. May not map directly to target system metrics.
Sprint.daysOff is an integer (aggregate person-days off), not a list of dates. Map to target system's capacity reduction mechanism if present.
Member.nonContributing flags members excluded from capacity calculations (e.g. EMs, PMs). Filter these out when computing fullCap.
WorkItem.dirtyFields lists field names edited locally since the last push, awaiting sync to the external system. Use this to implement selective push.
WorkItem.itemType is connector-supplied and read-only. It describes the issue type (Bug, Story, Task) in the external system.
WorkItem.build is the build/release label the item was pulled from in a prior release. Set by the connector; read-only.
WorkItem.descriptionFormat distinguishes plain text ('text', default) from sanitized connector-supplied HTML ('html'). Render accordingly.
Events are one-way markers (milestones); they do not block or constrain work items.
Release.connector holds non-secret routing config only; credentials stay in the local sync service. A null connector means the release is local and never syncs.
Release Tracker stores nested objects (workStreams, events, sprints) directly on Release; items are stored flat with a releaseId foreign key. Normalize as needed for target systems.
